description: Performance and Technical Enhancements (items 71-80): Virtual scrolling for long lists, image blur-up loading, predictive prefetching, IndexedDB for large storage, background sync, streaming search results with Suspense, route-based code splitting, memory-efficient audio, request deduplication, and stale-while-revalidate caching.
steps: 1. Create app/components/ui/VirtualList.tsx: implement virtual scrolling using Intersection Observer and a windowing approach - only render visible items plus buffer. Apply to track lists in search results and playlists with 100+ items.

2. Create app/components/ui/BlurUpImage.tsx: show blurred low-res thumbnail (using CSS blur filter on a tiny version) then load full resolution progressively. Replace Image usage in cards with this component.

3. Create lib/prefetch.ts and app/components/ui/PrefetchLink.tsx: on hover over links, prefetch the target page data. Use requestIdleCallback and router.prefetch() from Next.js. Apply to navigation links.

4. Create lib/indexeddb.ts: implement IndexedDB storage adapter for favorites, listening history, and collections (larger datasets). Provide get/set/delete with Promise-based API. Fall back to localStorage on error.
5. Create lib/background-sync.ts: queue favorite/unfavorite actions when offline (using navigator.onLine detection). When back online, replay queued actions. Store queue in localStorage.

6. Enhance search to use React Suspense streaming: in app/search/page.tsx, the existing Suspense boundaries already stream results. Ensure search result components use proper loading states and progressive rendering.
7. Apply dynamic imports for heavy pages: use next/dynamic with { ssr: false } or { loading } for analytics page components, collection editor, expanded player. Ensure route-based code splitting.

8. Enhance AudioPlayerContext.tsx: ensure Audio objects are properly released when switching tracks - call audio.src = '' and audio.load() before dereferencing. Track Audio instances to prevent memory leaks.

9. Create lib/request-cache.ts: implement in-flight request deduplication - if the same API URL is currently being fetched, return the existing Promise instead of making a new request. Use a Map of URL -> Promise.

10. Create lib/swr-cache.ts: implement stale-while-revalidate pattern - return cached results instantly from localStorage/memory, then fetch fresh data in background and update. Apply to iTunes search calls.
acceptance_criteria: Lists with 100+ items render smoothly with virtual scrolling
Images show blurred placeholder then load full resolution
Hovering links prefetches target page data
IndexedDB stores large datasets (favorites, history) reliably
Offline actions queue and sync when connection returns
Search results stream in progressively with Suspense boundaries
Heavy page components are code-split and lazy-loaded
Audio objects are properly cleaned up preventing memory leaks
Duplicate API requests share a single in-flight Promise
Cached results show instantly with background revalidation
verification: bun run lint passes with zero errors
bun run build succeeds with zero errors
findings: All 10 steps implemented successfully. Build and lint pass cleanly. The search page already had proper Suspense boundaries with streaming, so step 6 was verified as already complete. The favorites page uses dynamic imports for all heavy tab components (analytics, collections, artists, albums, history). AudioPlayerContext now properly releases Audio objects via src='' and load() before dereferencing, preventing memory leaks. The VirtualList uses ResizeObserver instead of setState in effect to avoid cascading renders lint error.
